Acta
v0.2.0 · generated from structured scenario data · regenerate when inputs change
Authority
Working surface only. Canonical state lives in Markdown/JSON and changes solely after explicit acceptance in chat.

Where should tag filtering and URL-state logic live, and which way should the DOM depend on it?

Node-only test runner (no DOM in tests) single ?tag= query param no-JS shows the full catalog 3-skill static fixture today matches existing pure-function idiom
A

Functional core, thin shell

Keep all logic as plain importable functions; DOM wiring is a few lines of glue.

src/catalog.mjs · src/url-tag.mjs · src/app.mjs
export function filterByTag(skills, tag) {
  if (!tag) return skills;
  return skills.filter((s) => s.tags.includes(tag));
}

export function parseTagFromSearch(search) {
  return new URLSearchParams(search).get("tag");
}

// app.mjs (browser-only glue, not imported by tests)
render(parseTagFromSearch(location.search));
  1. location.search
  2. parseTagFromSearch → tag | null
  3. filterByTag(skills, tag)
  4. replaceChildren(list items)
ProCon
zero new test dependenciesDOM glue has no direct test coverage
extends the existing visibleSkills() idiomeach future widget wires itself by hand
smallest review surface

Test seamnode --test imports both pure functions directly

Migrationpurely additive; static no-JS list already complete

Failure modes & change conditions

Failure modes:

  • manual DOM-sync mistake in the untested glue layer

Reconsider this path when:

  • a second widget must react to the same tag state
  • back/forward live-update becomes a hard requirement
B

Encapsulated custom element

The catalog becomes a self-contained <skill-catalog> element owning state and rendering.

src/skill-catalog-element.mjs
export class SkillCatalogElement extends HTMLElement {
  static get observedAttributes() { return ["tag"]; }
  connectedCallback() {
    const tag = new URLSearchParams(location.search).get("tag");
    if (tag) this.setAttribute("tag", tag); else this.#render();
  }
  attributeChangedCallback() { this.#render(); }
  #render() { /* filter + replaceChildren */ }
}
customElements.define("skill-catalog", SkillCatalogElement);
  1. location.search
  2. connectedCallback → tag attribute
  3. attributeChangedCallback
  4. #render() replaces light-DOM children
ProCon
strong encapsulationHTMLElement does not exist in plain Node
portable, reusable widgetneeds a hand-maintained static fallback inside the tag
back/forward handled inside the classlargest surface for a 3-item fixture

Test seamrequires a DOM shim, or extracting Path A's functions anyway

Migrationreplace the static <ul> with <skill-catalog> + fallback

Failure modes & change conditions

Failure modes:

  • attribute-change timing bugs invisible to the Node-only runner
  • tag-name registration collision

Reconsider this path when:

  • the catalog must ship as a widget to other pages or projects
C

Pub/sub URL-state store

A small store owns the URL and notifies any number of subscribers on every change.

src/url-tag-store.mjs · src/render.mjs
export function createTagStore(win = window) {
  const listeners = new Set();
  const readTag = () =>
    new URLSearchParams(win.location.search).get("tag");
  win.addEventListener("popstate", () =>
    listeners.forEach((fn) => fn(readTag())));
  return {
    getTag: readTag,
    setTag(tag) { /* pushState + notify */ },
    subscribe(fn) { listeners.add(fn); return () => listeners.delete(fn); },
  };
}
  1. setTag() or popstate
  2. store recomputes tag
  3. notifies every subscriber
  4. each subscriber calls filterByTag
ProCon
uniform back/forward handlingpub/sub machinery a single consumer does not need
clean fan-out to future consumerssubscriber-leak and ordering risks
testable via injected window stub

Test seamnode --test passes a plain object as win

Migrationadditive; extra bootstrap wiring at startup

Failure modes & change conditions

Failure modes:

  • dynamic consumer added without unsubscribe
  • subscriber ordering dependency

Reconsider this path when:

  • back/forward correctness becomes an explicit requirement
  • a second independent UI piece must react to tag changes

Same dimensions, all three paths

DimensionFunctional core, thin shellEncapsulated custom elementPub/sub URL-state store
New test dependency needed✓yes none✗no DOM shim, or defeats encapsulation✓yes none (plain object stub)
Respects Node-only test setup✓yes cleanly✗no conflicts, or redundant wrapper✓yes via injected win
Code volume for 3-skill fixture✓yes smallest✗no largest◐partly medium
Matches existing repo idiom✓yes extends visibleSkills()✗no introduces classes◐partly wraps pure filterByTag
Back/forward navigation◐partly not by default, addable✓yes built in✓yes built in
Fit for stated scope✓yes best fit✗no over-built✗no over-built
Recommendation — after the comparisonFunctional core, thin shell: The only shape with no tension against the Node-only test constraint: logic stays in plain functions testable exactly like visibleSkills(), with no DOM shim and no new dependency. B collapses into a wrapper around A once logic is extracted for testing; C answers requirements nobody has stated.
